Section 1
def count_word_occurrences(text, word):
    import re
    text_lower = text.lower()
    word_lower = word.lower()
    pattern = r'\b' + re.escape(word_lower) + r'\b'
    matches = re.findall(pattern, text_lower)
    return len(matches)

Section 2
The logic uses regular expressions to find whole words only. It converts both inputs to lowercase for case insensitivity. The escape function prevents special characters from breaking the pattern. This ensures accurate counting regardless of punctuation or case variations.

Section 3
Testing the example provided yields the expected result of two. The word freedom appears twice in the sample text. One instance starts with a capital letter while the other is lowercase. The function treats both as identical matches.
